description.en: Out-of-range values have invalid values removed, leaving a valid Flag value: >>> from enum import Flag, CONFORM, auto >>> class ConformFlag(Flag, boundary=CONFORM): ... RED = auto() ... GREEN = auto() ... BLUE = auto() ... >>> ConformFlag(2**2 + 2**4) <ConformFlag.BLUE: 4>

excerpt.es: Out-of-range values have invalid values removed, leaving a valid Flag value: >>> from enum import Flag, CONFORM, auto >>> class ConformFlag(Flag, boundary=CONFORM): ... RED = auto() ... GREEN = auto()...
excerpt.en: Out-of-range values have invalid values removed, leaving a valid Flag value: >>> from enum import Flag, CONFORM, auto >>> class ConformFlag(Flag, boundary=CONFORM): ... RED = auto() ... GREEN = auto()...

description.en: Ensure that there are no missing values between the lowest-valued member and the highest-valued member: >>> from enum import Enum, verify, CONTINUOUS >>> @verify(CONTINUOUS) ... class Color(Enum): ... RED = 1 ... GREEN = 2 ... BLUE = 5 Traceback (most recent call last): ... ValueError: invalid enum 'Color': missing values 3, 4

excerpt.es: Ensure that there are no missing values between the lowest-valued member and the highest-valued member: >>> from enum import Enum, verify, CONTINUOUS >>> @verify(CONTINUOUS) ... class Color(Enum): ......
excerpt.en: Ensure that there are no missing values between the lowest-valued member and the highest-valued member: >>> from enum import Enum, verify, CONTINUOUS >>> @verify(CONTINUOUS) ... class Color(Enum): ......

description.en: EnumCheck contains the options used by the verify() decorator to ensure various constraints; failed constraints result in a ValueError. UNIQUE¶ Ensure that each value has only one name: >>> from enum import Enum, verify, UNIQUE >>> @verify(UNIQUE) ... class Color(Enum): ... RED = 1 ... GREEN = 2 ... BLUE = 3 ... CRIMSON = 1 Traceback (most recent call last): ... ValueError: aliases found in <enum 'Color'>: CRIMSON -> RED CONTINUOUS¶ Ensure that there are no missing values between the lowest-v...
